summary: Crisp soundboard recording with a solid ’92 show highlighted by Dark Star, Morning Dew and a memorable Corrina
review: The board feed is exceptionally clear – vocals are crisp and the instruments sit nicely apart – making this one of the cleaner ’92 tapes.  Musically the night shines on a dark‑and‑textured Dark Star, a peppy Morning Dew and a standout Corrina (despite a few dissenting listeners), while Deal and Big River also get strong nods.  Overall the performance is solid with a few mixed feelings about the Satisfaction encore.

ai_rating.rationale: 23 reviews give an average of ~3.3 stars; most praise the sound quality and key songs, but a handful of negative comments (especially about Satisfaction and Corrina) keep the overall rating in the mixed‑positive range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clean board feed with great instrument separation; occasional slight shakiness on a few rows but no hiss or dropouts reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Setlist is generally praised as solid and well‑balanced; the second set receives a note that its song choices leave a little to be desired, yet the playing remains strong throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for rich, thoughtful guitar tone on Dark Star and Corrina; some reviewers note he seemed tired or under the influence later in the night.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as sounding "phat" with a fat bass line on Corrina; overall bass work is highlighted positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboard interplay called finely tuned, honky‑tonk piano and MIDI praised; strong synergy with Jerry noted.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bruce’s last tour noted for energizing the band; drum/space sections called scintillating, though one reviewer missed the kick drum.
